titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the May Day Rally and Yale Collection
May Day Rally and Yale Collection
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig and the staff of Manuscripts and Archives

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 31, 2020 at 07:32
Finding aid revision description not supplied.
General note added with missing item information
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.unittitle: May Day Rally and Yale collection
did.physdesc: (21 boxes, 51 audiocassettes, one compact disc and 2 film reels.)
did.abstract: The materials consist of correspondence, press releases, oral history transcripts, objects, and printed material documenting Yale student involvement in the 1970 May Day rally in New Haven, Connecticut and published materials collected by the donor as a stringer for the "New York Times" related to Yale's student strike, the anti-war movement, and the Black Panthers trial in New Haven. Also included are publications related to anti-war activities nationally.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Black Panther "solidarity" shirt and silk screen banner gift of Ms. Karla Forsythe, 2013. Films of May Day activities from the New Haven Green gift of Michael Stickrod, 2016. Gift of Ben Krasney, 2018. Accession 2020-A-0022 was donated by Henry Lawrence, Class of 1968.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 41 (YRG 41), Records documenting student life.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: May Day Rally and Yale Collection (RU 86). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Yale University records are arranged and described at the accession level by the creating office. The University Archives creates collection level descriptive records, but typically does no further arrangement and description at the accession level.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: May Day weekend took place May 1-3, 1970 as a rally to protest the murder trial in New Haven of Black Panther party chairman Bobby Seale and seven other party members. Normal academic activities were suspended so that Yale students, faculty, and staff could assist in preparing the campus for the rally. Yale University opened its doors to demonstrators by offering shelter, food, day care, and first aid throughout the weekend. Approximately 15,000 people attended the first day of rallies without significant disruption or disorder. Fewer remained the following days, and academic activities resumed by Monday May 4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The materials consist of correspondence, press releases, oral history transcripts, objects, and printed material documenting Yale student involvement in the 1970 May Day rally in New Haven, Connecticut and published materials collected by the donor as a stringer for the "New York Times" related to Yale's student strike, the anti-war movement, and the Black Panthers trial in New Haven. Also included are publications related to anti-war activities nationally. Some posters and broadsides in Accession 1971-A-004 that were originally located in box 2 are now located in box 6.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by accession.

odd.head: General
odd.p: 1917-M-004, box 4, folder "Miscellaneous statements and releases by community groups, New Haven Police Department, and Others" reported missing in 2016.

odd.head: General
odd.p: 1917-M-004, box 4, folder "Miscellaneous statements and releases by community groups, New Haven Police Department, and Others" reported missing in 2016.

did.unittitle: Black Panthers - Law students" and family memoranda
Yale Daily News - Clippings and selected issues
[Poem] Ginsberg, Allen. May King's Prophecy. [New Haven], May Day Noon 1970. 2 p. (typescript) with stamp
New Haven Police Department - memoranda, circulars
Strike Committee - Notes and miscellaneous materials from meetings (2 folders)
[Essay] Samuels, Ed. Dear Mom and Dad . An account of Mayday at Yale. New Haven, [1970]. 114 p. (Typescript, electrostatic copy)
Statements, releases, et cetera - faculty and administration
Statements, releases, et cetera - students and others
Miscellaneous statements and releases by community groups, New Haven Police Dept. and others

odd.head: General
odd.p: 1971-M-004, box 4, folder "Miscellaneous statements and releases by community groups, New Haven Police Department, and Others" reported missing in 2016.

did.unittitle: National Student Strike - Releases, issues of the newspaper nos. 8, 9, May 12, 14
Student strikes at other colleges and universities - leaflets, bulletins, etc.
Other controversy at Yale - arranged topically:
Shockley and report on Free speech, 1974
Yale Institute for Social Sciences - Proposal: student comment and other discussion, 1970
Lynd, Staughton - Letters of Yale Class of 1926
Publications distributed at Yale on political issues, ca. 1970, arranged topically:
Political and economic groups - various protest actions
Press release, correspondence, and paper on Black Power
Press release, correspondence, and paper on Black Power
Interviews of people involved in the 1970 May Day Strike

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

did.unittitle: Live Reporting of Rally and Press conference

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A recording quality is poor.

The beginning of Side B contains 13 minutes of live reporting for multiple unidentified reporters describing the scene during the May Day rally on the Green. Beginning at 13:52, the remainder of Side B records a press conference between Kingman Brewster Jr. and a number of unidentified reporters.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access.

did.unittitle: Correspondence concerning the 1970 May Day Strike
Materials concerning the 1970 May Day strike
Memos to Faculty and Students; Correspondence
Materials documenting the Black Panthers and the May Day Rally
Newspapers regarding rally and the Black Panthers
Newspapers regarding rally and the Black Panthers
Newspapers regarding Black Panther Party and New Haven Panther Defense Committee

May Day "Strikes" tee-shirt made in Berkeley College print shop

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

did.unittitle: National anti-war and student strike publications and brochures
Anti-war and Black Panther activities-New Haven
Anti-war and May Day publications at Yale
The Black Panther Black Community News service
Black Panther statements, ephemera and news clippings
Black Panther "solidarity" shirt and silk screen banner

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession consists of two items: a button-down shirt silk-screened on the back with the word, "Solidarity," as well as an illustration featuring the Black Panther Party logo and a bulldog; and a blue cloth banner silk screened with two Black Panther Party logos and the phrase, "POLITICAL PRISONERS OF USA FASCISM." Both the shirt and the banner were donated by Karla Forsythe, a 1971 Yale College graduate.

did.unittitle: "Political prisoners of U.S.A. fascism" silk screen banner
Letter on the Black Panther trial

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

did.unittitle: Letter on the Black Panther trial

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unidentified typescript letter describing the events leading up to, and during the trial of the Black Panthers in New Haven.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession contains two short films that document activities from the May Day Rally that took place on the New Haven Green. The films were created by Frank Gardner, a New Haven-based film maker.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the original film reels is restricted. Researchers must use the available digital surrogates.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Color film with no sound. Run time is two minutes and ten seconds.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Color film with no sound. Run time is eight minutes and forty-two seconds.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession includes a typescript of "Nature and history of the Black Panther Party," additional copies of "Strike Newspaper," and a copy of "The Yale Graduate Professional."

did.unittitle: "Nature and history of the Black Panther Party" typescript
